nativeRange.habitat: Trade cultivar selected from Rotala rotundifolia stock for intensified pink-red coloration. Wild type-form habitat: marshes, rice paddies, and the muddy margins of slow-moving streams across temperate and tropical Asia.

decorPreferences: Nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil) and iron-rich root tabs
High light (PAR 50+) needed for the 'Colorata' name to live up to itself
CO2 injection strongly recommended for compact form + intense color
Soft, slightly acidic water deepens the pink/magenta hue
Regular trimming maintains tight bushy growth and prevents legginess

summary: A selected cultivar of Rotala rotundifolia bred for intense pink-to-magenta coloration. Carries the standard R. rotundifolia growth habit but commits more strongly to red top growth under high light, CO2, and iron — making it a popular background or trimmed-bush midground plant in high-tech aquascapes.
careNotes:
Rotala 'Colorata' is a trade cultivar widely treated as a selected form of Rotala rotundifolia (parent entry fw-plant-046). Some retailers and aquarists alternatively position it closer to Rotala wallichii or as a hybrid cultivar of uncertain lineage; the consensus across most aquascape sources is the R. rotundifolia clade. Care closely mirrors the parent species, but the cultivar's coloration genuinely depends on high light (50+ PAR), CO2 injection, iron supplementation, and ideally low nitrate to push the deepest pink-red color into the new growth. Under low light or low CO2, leaves stay olive-green and the cultivar becomes indistinguishable from the standard 'Green' form. Background stem placement; trim regularly to maintain bushy density and to propagate via cuttings. Soft acidic water (pH 5.5–6.5) intensifies pink-magenta tones compared to the wider parent species' tolerance. Consider also: fw-plant-046 (Rotala rotundifolia), fw-plant-081 (Rotala indica), fw-plant-162 (Rotala macrandra), fw-plant-161 (Rotala H'Ra). 

NOTE: This entry was written from genus/parent baselines after the original research dispatch hung on upstream sources. Reviewer should verify the cultivar lineage and color-response specifics against Tropica or 2HR Aquarist when the URL cleanup pass runs.

primary.name: Rotala rotundifolia parent entry (fw-plant-046)
primary.notes: Genus-baseline inheritance from researched cryptocoryne-rotundifolia parent. Dispatch attempt hung on upstream retailer sources; manifest URL cleanup pending.
